verdict.answer_first: Tie: both state the actionable cause immediately; A says "POST triggers a CORS preflight and your server isn't answering it", B says "POST is triggering a CORS preflight and your GET isn't".
verdict.decisiveness: B slightly: it commits directly to the server fix with "make it respond to the OPTIONS request (status 200/204)" while A adds extra diagnostic/testing detail.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on right now: it gives the preflight cause, the required OPTIONS response status, exact headers, and credentials caveat in a shorter path.
